query:
Research the company "ab inbev".

Research the company's recent operational and financial performance: (1) Latest quarterly or annual revenue and margin metrics - look for recent misses, shortfalls, or guidance changes (2) Recent CEO changes or new leadership appointments (3) Evidence of strategic reviews or restructuring announcements (4) Production/manufacturing capacity data, product mix, or platform launches (5) Distribution and pricing strategy signals (6) Specific mentions of cross-functional challenges, siloed operations, or margin compression despite functional wins (7) Industry sector and business model (B2C consumer goods, automotive, manufacturing, etc.) (8) Company size and scale indicators (Fortune 500 status, revenue ranges, regional presence) (9) Media/marketing spend and promotional activity (10) Any recent analyst reports or earnings call transcripts discussing structural inefficiencies or P&L gaps.

Focus on the last 6 months. Return a comprehensive summary with specific facts, numbers, and dates.
answer:
**Executive Summary**
AB InBev demonstrated strong financial momentum in late 2025 and early 2026, highlighted by a record Q1 2026 EPS of $0.97 and a 12% reported revenue increase. The company is actively restructuring its U.S. manufacturing footprint through facility closures and a $600 million investment plan, while driving product mix shifts toward high-margin non-alcoholic and "Beyond Beer" categories. Recent months have also seen significant executive turnover across regional leadership and the Board of Directors.

### Financial Performance & Guidance
AB InBev has posted consecutive earnings beats over the last two quarters, driven by premiumization and revenue management.

*   **Q1 2026 Results:** The company reported Q1 2026 revenue of $15.267 billion, topping consensus estimates of approximately $14.7 billion [Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD): Q1 2026 Earnings Beat Highlights Premiumization Strength](https://tickeron.com/blogs/anheuser-busch-inbev-bud-q1-2026-earnings-beat-highlights-premiumization-strength-13148/). This represented a 5.8% organic increase and a 12.0% reported increase year-over-year [Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD): Q1 2026 Earnings Beat Highlights Premiumization Strength](https://tickeron.com/blogs/anheuser-busch-inbev-bud-q1-2026-earnings-beat-highlights-premiumization-strength-13148/). Underlying EPS reached a record first-quarter level of $0.97, a 20.8% year-over-year increase that beat the $0.91 consensus [AB InBev Delivers Record Q1 2026 EPS on Strong Megabrand and Digital Growth - The Globe and Mail](https://www.theglobeandmail.com/investing/markets/stocks/BUD/pressreleases/1742473/ab-inbev-delivers-record-q1-2026-eps-on-strong-megabrand-and-digital-growth/). Normalized EBITDA was $5.437 billion with a stable margin of 35.6% [Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD): Q1 2026 Earnings Beat Highlights Premiumization Strength](https://tickeron.com/blogs/anheuser-busch-inbev-bud-q1-2026-earnings-beat-highlights-premiumization-strength-13148/], while the operating margin recovered to 26.7% [Anheuser-Busch InBev Q1 2026 Earnings: Record EPS and 12% Revenue Growth | TIKR.com](https://www.tikr.com/blog/anheuser-busch-inbev-q1-2026-earnings-record-eps-and-12-revenue-growth).
*   **Q4 & FY 2025 Results:** Full-year 2025 revenue was $59.32 billion (a 0.8% reported decline but a 2.0% organic increase), with a normalized EBITDA margin of 35.8%, representing a 101 basis point organic expansion [Anheuser-Busch InBev Beats Q4 2025 Earnings Estimates](https://www.valuethemarkets.com/news/anheuser-busch-inbev-beats-q4-2025-earnings-estimates), [AB InBev Annual Report 2025 (SEC Filing)](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1668717/000119312526049841/d891969dex992.htm). Q4 2025 revenue was $15.55 billion; while one source noted this beat market expectations of $15.51 billion, another reported it missed the Zacks Consensus Estimate [Anheuser-Busch InBev Beats Q4 2025 Earnings Estimates](https://www.valuethemarkets.com/news/anheuser-busch-inbev-beats-q4-2025-earnings-estimates), [AB InBev Q4 Earnings Beat on Business Momentum, Revenues Miss - The Globe and Mail](https://www.theglobeandmail.com/investing/markets/stocks/CABGY/pressreleases/188609/ab-inbev-q4-earnings-beat-on-business-momentum-revenues-miss/). Q4 EPS of $0.95 beat the $0.91 consensus, though the normalized EBITDA margin contracted by 10 basis points year-over-year to 35.2% [Anheuser-Busch InBev Beats Q4 2025 Earnings Estimates](https://www.valuethemarkets.com/news/anheuser-busch-inbev-beats-q4-2025-earnings-estimates), [AB InBev Q4 Earnings Beat on Business Momentum, Revenues Miss - The Globe and Mail](https://www.theglobeandmail.com/investing/markets/stocks/CABGY/pressreleases/188609/ab-inbev-q4-earnings-beat-on-business-momentum-revenues-miss/).
*   **FY 2026 Guidance:** AB InBev reaffirmed its FY 2026 guidance, projecting 4-8% organic EBITDA growth [Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD): Q1 2026 Earnings Beat Highlights Premiumization Strength](https://tickeron.com/blogs/anheuser-busch-inbev-bud-q1-2026-earnings-beat-highlights-premiumization-strength-13148/). The company expects net capital expenditures between $3.5 billion and $4.0 billion, with a normalized effective tax rate of 26-28% [Anheuser-busch Inbev (BUD) Earnings Dates, Call Summary & Reports - TipRanks.com](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/bud/earnings).

### Strategic Restructuring & Manufacturing Capacity
The company is executing a major consolidation of its U.S. manufacturing footprint while expanding capacity in emerging markets and vertical supply chains.

*   **U.S. Consolidation & Investment:** AB InBev is closing breweries in Fairfield, California, and Merrimack, New Hampshire, in early 2026, and selling its Newark, New Jersey, facility to the Goodman Group [AB InBev to shut two US factories and sell another to consolidate operations](https://www.foodbev.com/news/ab-inbev-to-shut-two-us-factories-and-sell-another-to-consolidate-operations). Concurrently, the company is investing $600 million across 2025 and 2026 to upgrade remaining U.S. operations [AB InBev to invest 600 million dollars in US manufacturing](https://www.belganewsagency.eu/ab-inbev-to-invest-600-million-dollars-in-us-manufacturing). This includes opening 15 new technical skills training centers to upskill over 90% of its manufacturing workforce over five years [Anheuser-Busch Increases Investment in U.S. Manufacturing to $600 Million | MarketScreener](https://www.marketscreener.com/news/anheuser-busch-increases-investment-in-u-s-manufacturing-to-600-million-ce7f59d8d18eff26).
*   **Supply Chain Verticalization:** The company is reacquiring a 49.9% stake in its U.S. aluminum can manufacturing business (seven facilities across six states) from Apollo Global Management [AB InBev regains stake in aluminum can business - Recycling Today](https://www.recyclingtoday.com/news/ab-inbev-anheuser-bush-can-making-repurchase-apollo-2026/).
*   **International Expansion:** In India, AB InBev is investing $121 million in a new greenfield brewery in Unnao, Uttar Pradesh (scheduled for 2025), and an additional $250 million to expand brewing operations over the next 2–3 years [Beer Market Share, Size, Growth and Industry Trend to 2032](https://www.marknteladvisors.com/research-library/beer-market.html).

### Product Mix, Pricing & Digital Platforms
AB InBev is successfully shifting its product mix toward higher-margin categories, which drove a 4.5% increase in revenue per hectoliter in Q1 2026 [AB InBev (BUD) boosts Q1 2026 profit and EBITDA on stronger mix](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/BUD/6-k-anheuser-busch-in-bev-sa-nv-current-report-foreign-issuer-7489e6e5566f.html).

*   **Beyond Beer:** The "Beyond Beer" portfolio saw revenue grow by 37% in Q1 2026, led by Cutwater Spirits and Flying Fish [AB InBev Reports First Quarter 2026 Results | AFP.com](https://www.afp.com/en/infos/ab-inbev-reports-first-quarter-2026-results). To support this, the company invested $16 million to expand its Los Angeles brewery for Cutwater and Nütrl production [Anheuser-Busch invests $16M in facility to boost drinks ‘beyond beer’ | Food Dive](https://www.fooddive.com/news/anheuser-busch-invests-16m-in-los-angeles-brewery-cutwater-nutrl-RTD-canned-cocktails-ab-inbev/728395/), and is reportedly acquiring alcoholic punch manufacturer BeatBox [AB InBev investit dans le segment "Beyond Beer" - La Libre](https://www.lalibre.be/economie/entreprises-startup/2025/11/19/ab-inbev-investit-dans-le-segment-beyond-beer-O7IST7ABD5EFFEYBK72QE6LDBI/).
*   **Non-Alcoholic Growth:** Non-alcohol beer revenue grew 34% in 2025, carrying profit margins estimated to be 20% higher than traditional beer [Alcohol-free offers growth driver for AB InBev, Heineken, Carlsberg](https://www.beveragedaily.com/Article/2026/03/27/alcohol-free-offers-growth-driver-for-ab-inbev-heineken-carlsberg/). The company launched Michelob Ultra Zero in January 2025 [AB InBev in 2024 Unfiltered: Resilience, Innovation, and Ambition – Drinks Times](https://www.drinks-times.com/2025/02/natxub71d231/) and invested $37.4 million across its Jacksonville and Los Angeles facilities to boost Michelob Ultra production and can formats [AB InBev to pour $30 million in US brewery to boost Michelob Ultra beer production | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/retail-consumer/ab-inbev-pour-30-million-us-brewery-boost-michelob-ultra-beer-production-2026-01-06/), [The Bull Case For Anheuser-Busch InBev (ENXTBR:ABI) Could Change Following Major LA Brewery Expansion Announcement](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/bull-case-anheuser-busch-inbev-123800772.html).
*   **B2B Digital Platform:** The company's BEES platform was live in 29 markets as of March 31, 2026, and now captures 72% of AB InBev's total revenues [AB InBev Reports First Quarter 2026 Results | AFP.com](https://www.afp.com/en/infos/ab-inbev-reports-first-quarter-2026-results).

### Executive Leadership Changes
Several key leadership transitions occurred between December 2025 and April 2026:

*   **Board of Directors:** Chairman Martin J. Barrington announced his retirement effective April 29, 2026. William F. Gifford Jr., former CEO of Altria, was named to succeed him as a director, pending shareholder approval [Anheuser-Busch InBev SA/NV (ABI) Leadership & Management Team Analysis - Simply Wall St](https://simplywall.st/stocks/be/food-beverage-tobacco/ebr-abi/anheuser-busch-inbev-shares/management), [Martin J. Barrington Stopt Als Voorzitter AB Inbev • De Bestuurder](https://debestuurder.be/martin-j-barrington-stopt-als-voorzitter-ab-inbev/).
*   **UK & Ireland:** Brian Perkins exited his role as CEO of Budweiser Brewing Group UK&I on March 12, 2026, for a new internal role. As of that date, no replacement had been confirmed [Budweiser Brewing Group UK&I CEO exits for new role in AB InBev](https://www.foodmanufacture.co.uk/Article/2026/03/12/budweiser-brewing-group-uki-ceo-exits-for-new-role-in-ab-inbev/).
*   **Germany:** Jannik Weitzl departed as Country Director in December 2025, succeeded by Guilherme Machado De Lima in February 2026 [Germany: AB InBev reshuffles leadership amid performance pressure | inside.beer](https://www.inside.beer/news/detail/germany-ab-inbev-reshuffles-leadership-amid-performance-pressure).
*   **International Breweries:** Effective March 1, 2026, Carlos Coutino transitioned from Managing Director of International Breweries Plc (Nigeria) to MD for Honduras and El Salvador. Nicholas Kade succeeded him in Nigeria [International Breweries Announces Changes in Executive Management – THISDAYLIVE](https://www.thisdaylive.com/2026/01/19/international-breweries-announces-changes-in-executive-management/).

### Gaps and Limitations
The provided research findings did not contain information regarding the following requested areas:
*   Specific mentions of cross-functional challenges, siloed operations, or margin compression despite functional wins.
*   Explicit definitions of the company's industry sector, business model, or Fortune 500 status (beyond standard revenue figures and its identity as a brewer/B2B platform operator).
*   Data on media/marketing spend and promotional activity.
*   Recent analyst reports or earnings call transcripts specifically discussing structural inefficiencies or P&L gaps.
